schema_version: v0 — skill-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: Session close: write a slate to CairnCommons/slates/ capturing the state of work at the boundary — in-flight, next direction, open threads — for a clean resume.
why: Continuity that lives only in the conversation dies at the context boundary. Precipitating it into a commons artifact makes it durable AND shareable — it is knowledge the system would lose, not just CC. Closes the fifth feedback loop's save-half; the slate carries its open threads forward so nothing settled is re-derived (Law 1) and nothing unfinished is silently dropped.
invoke: /saveslate  (at session close, or before a compaction boundary)
relation_to_memory: CC's ~/.claude memory is personal + cross-session; a slate is the in-commons, shareable continuity record. They can agree; when they differ, the slate says so.
traces_to: Law 1 (carry the settled forward, don't re-derive it)
the learning-loop keystone (a slate is best-guess-at-boundary, non-terminal)
Law 7 (open threads are recorded, not collapsed into a false 'done')
owner: the skills charter gates writes; Akien ratifies changes (signature gate)
proof: skill node → done only when cairnmap recompiles green (derivation gate). IOU until cairnmap exists — tracked in proofs/.
provenance: converged in the founding design conversation; absorbed the old day-close skill (MAP.md 'Workflow + starting skills', 2026-07-15).
